UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile in the Ummy app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

avatarUrl.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

bio.description: A short biography or description of the user.

interests.description: An array of strings representing the user's interests.

ChatRoom.title: ChatRoom
ChatRoom.description: Represents a voice chat room in the Ummy app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat room.

name.description: The name of the chat room.

description.description: A description of the chat room's topic.

ownerId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatRoom).  The user ID of the chat room owner.

moderatorIds.description: References to UserProfiles. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatRoom). An array of user IDs representing the chat room moderators.

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the chat room was created.

tags.description: References to Tags. (Relationship: Tag N:N ChatRoom). An array of tag ID

FriendRequest.title: FriendRequest
FriendRequest.description: Represents a friend request between users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the friend request.

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FriendRequest). The user ID of the sender.

receiverId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N FriendRequest). The user ID of the receiver.

status.description: The status of the friend request (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'rejected').

createdAt.description: Timestamp of when the friend request was created.

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Represents a text message sent within a chat room.

id.description: Unique identifier for the chat message.

chatRoomId.description: Reference to ChatRoom. (Relationship: ChatRoom 1:N ChatMessage). The chat room ID where the message was sent.

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatMessage). The user ID of the sender.

content.description: The content of the chat message.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the message was sent.

Gift.title: Gift
Gift.description: Represents a virtual gift that can be sent between users.

value.description: The value of the gift (e.g., in virtual currency).

UserGift.title: UserGift
UserGift.description: Represents a gift sent from one user to another.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user gift transaction.

senderId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserGift). The user ID of the sender.

receiverId.description: Reference to UserProfile. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N UserGift). The user ID of the receiver.

giftId.description: Reference to Gift. (Relationship: Gift 1:N UserGift). The gift ID.

timestamp.description: Timestamp of when the gift was sent.

message.description: An optional message from the sender.

Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a tag for a chatroom.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Path-based ownership ensures only the user can modify their profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Stores chat room information, including the owner and moderators for access control. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' and 'moderatorIds' for authorization independence.

params.name: chatRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat room.

definition.description: Stores friend request information. Enables listing and securing friend requests based on sender and receiver IDs.

params.name: friendRequestId
params.description: The unique identifier of the friend request.

definition.description: Stores chat messages within a chat room.

params.name: chatRoomId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat room.

params.name: chatMessageId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chat message.

definition.description: Stores gift information. This is a top-level collection that requires special access control based on admin roles (not explicitly defined in schemas).

params.name: giftId
params.description: The unique identifier of the gift.

definition.description: Stores information about gifts sent between users.

params.name: userGiftId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user gift transaction.

definition.description: Stores tag information for chat rooms.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique identifier of the tag.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support a voice chat room application (like Ummy) with a focus on user profiles, chat rooms, friend requests, chat messages, and virtual gifting. It prioritizes authorization independence and security through denormalization and structural segregation.

**Authorization Independence:** The structure avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization data. For example, the `ChatRoom` documents include the `ownerId` which can be used in rules without fetching the user's document. Furthermore, fields like `moderatorIds` are embedded within the `ChatRoom` document. This avoids the need to fetch user roles from separate collections.

**Structural Segregation:** User-specific data (e.g., profile) is stored under `/users/{userId}`, ensuring clear ownership. Chat rooms are stored in a top-level `/chatRooms` collection, but each document contains `ownerId` and `moderatorIds`, enabling fine-grained access control without the need for collection group queries.

**QAPs (Rules Are Not Filters):** The structure supports secure list operations. Listing chat rooms can be secured based on membership or ownership, thanks to the denormalized `ownerId` and `moderatorIds` fields within each chat room document. Friend requests are segregated under `/friendRequests` and can be queried and secured based on the `senderId` and `receiverId`.

**Access Modeling:** Path-based ownership is used for user profiles (`/users/{userId}/profile`). Collaborative data (chat rooms) includes an `ownerId` and `moderatorIds` for access control. Global roles are not explicitly implemented, as the authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` and data within the documents. If roles were a requirement, a separate `/roles_admin/{uid}` collection could be added to denote admin roles.

**Invariants:** The structure supports data integrity. Timestamps (`createdAt`, `timestamp`) are included in relevant entities to track creation and modification times. Ownership is enforced through the `ownerId` field in `ChatRoom` documents, ensuring that only the owner can modify certain aspects of the chat room.
